etymology.description: semantic 日(日) (rì, "sun") + phonetic 青(青) (qīng) -> clear weather
etymology.interpretation: The sun 日(日) (rì, "sun") represents the element of daytime or light, and 青(青) (qīng) provides the phonetic sound. Together, they form 晴, indicating clear or fine weather, as sunlight naturally suggests clarity and brightness in the sky.

examples.chinese: 今天的天气很晴(今天的天氣很晴)。
早上的天空是晴的(早上的天空是晴的)，真美丽(真美麗)。
examples.pinyin: Zǎoshang de tiānkōng shì qíng de, zhēn měilì.
examples.english: The morning sky is clear, truly beautiful.

examples.chinese: 雨后总是会放晴(雨後總是會放晴)。
examples.pinyin: Yǔ hòu zǒng shì huì fàng qíng.
examples.english: It always clears up after the rain.
